rag_rules: Keep type-scoped contract content in component type owners, and keep implementation-dependent content in component implementation owners.
Any inserted implementation-level binding must stay after the subcomponents and connections it depends on.
Fusion must preserve existing architecture and use only already visible model elements.
generic_rules: Merge AGREE text into the correct owner scope without moving or rewriting unrelated AADL declarations.
Prefer extending an existing AGREE annex for that owner; otherwise add a new valid annex block.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annex blocks use `annex agree {** ... **};` delimiters.
Implementation-scope content belongs inside an implementation body, not a type body.
anti_patterns: Do not place subcomponent paths or other implementation-only references in a type-scoped annex.
Do not invent ports, data types, properties, connections, or other structural elements to make inserted content fit.
irrelevant_topics: Boolean expression examples that teach clause logic rather than owner scope or fusion behavior.
Validator-specific repair advice about deleting unresolved property or identifier references.
